customers.name: Cloud Application Builder
customers.description: A software engineering, product engineering, or application platform lead building cloud-native products with managed runtimes, APIs, data services, AI services, and automated delivery paths.
careAbout: Starting services quickly with the right runtime, database, messaging, and API patterns
Keeping application teams productive without taking on undifferentiated infrastructure operations
Adding AI, eventing, and managed data capabilities without fragmenting the architecture
Getting clear debugging, deployment, security, and cost feedback before production issues grow
winsThem: Opinionated but flexible cloud application paths across containers, serverless, app hosting, and APIs
Managed databases, eventing, secrets, observability, and docs that reduce integration effort
Self-service environments with secure defaults and cost visibility
A clear path from prototype to production scale without switching platforms
theirFear: A simple prototype becomes a brittle production architecture
Service choice overload slows delivery and creates inconsistent patterns across teams
Runtime, data, security, and cost ownership is unclear when incidents happen

jobsToBeDone.name: Build and release cloud-native applications quickly
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Create, deploy, expose, and operate application services using managed runtime, API, database, messaging, configuration, and observability services.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Application teams ship production features faster while staying inside secure and observable cloud patterns.

steps.description: Choose containers, serverless, or managed app hosting based on workload shape, team skills, scaling profile, and operational expectations.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Container and Kubernetes Platform
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports portable containerized workloads and cluster-based application operations.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Serverless Functions
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports event-driven functions and bursty application components without server management.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Managed App Hosting
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides managed application hosting for web, API, and service workloads.

media.title: Cloud Application Builder JTBD step 1: Select the right runtime pattern
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Build and release cloud-native applications quickly": Select the right runtime pattern.

steps.description: Wire application APIs, databases, events, secrets, and networking with clear security and ownership boundaries.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: API Gateway and Service Integration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Publishes APIs with authentication, throttling, and developer access controls.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Managed Databases
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides managed persistence without requiring teams to operate database infrastructure.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Event Bus and Messaging
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Decouples application components through queues, topics, events, and replayable messages.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Secrets and Configuration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Keeps secrets, certificates, and runtime configuration out of application code.

media.title: Cloud Application Builder JTBD step 2: Connect application services safely
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Build and release cloud-native applications quickly": Connect application services safely.

steps.description: Monitor deployments, investigate failures, manage cost, and improve reliability before customers feel the blast radius.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Monitoring and Observability
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides logs, metrics, traces, alerts, and service health views.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: FinOps and Commercial Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows cost attribution and optimization signals for application teams.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Developer Documentation and SDKs
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Gives developers examples, SDKs, tutorials, and reference patterns for repeatable delivery.

media.title: Cloud Application Builder JTBD step 3: Operate releases with feedback
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Build and release cloud-native applications quickly": Operate releases with feedback.

media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Build and release cloud-native applications quickly" for customer "Cloud Application Builder".

jobsToBeDone.name: Add data and AI capabilities to product experiences
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Use managed analytics, object storage, AI platforms, accelerated compute, and APIs to add intelligent product features without building a separate data platform first.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Product teams can test and scale AI-powered experiences while keeping data, model, and cost controls visible.

steps.description: Capture product events, store application data, and make it available for analytics or AI workflows.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Object Storage
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Stores product data, files, logs, and training inputs durably.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Analytics and Data Platform
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides managed analytics, transformation, and query paths for application data.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Event Bus and Messaging
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Streams product events into analytics and automation workflows.

media.title: Cloud Application Builder JTBD step 1: Prepare application data for intelligence
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Add data and AI capabilities to product experiences": Prepare application data for intelligence.

steps.description: Use managed AI services and accelerator capacity to prototype, evaluate, and serve model-backed features.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: AI and Machine Learning Platform
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides model development, training, inference, MLOps, and managed AI service access.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Accelerated Compute
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supplies accelerator capacity for training, tuning, or high-throughput inference.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: API Gateway and Service Integration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Exposes AI-backed capabilities through stable product APIs.

media.title: Cloud Application Builder JTBD step 2: Build and serve AI-backed features
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Add data and AI capabilities to product experiences": Build and serve AI-backed features.

steps.description: Apply identity, monitoring, usage, and cost controls so AI features can survive production review.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Identity and Access Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Applies role and service identity controls around application and AI access.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Monitoring and Observability
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Monitors latency, failures, and usage behavior for AI-backed product flows.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: FinOps and Commercial Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Tracks training, inference, storage, and analytics costs back to owners and features.

media.title: Cloud Application Builder JTBD step 3: Control trust and economics
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Add data and AI capabilities to product experiences": Control trust and economics.

media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Add data and AI capabilities to product experiences" for customer "Cloud Application Builder".

customerJourneyStories.name: Cloud Application Builder product delivery journey
customerJourneyStories.summary: An application builder moves from service idea to production cloud workload, then expands the product with managed data and AI capabilities.

stages.narrative: A product team needs to launch a new service or AI-backed feature faster than internal infrastructure processes allow.

media.title: Cloud Application Builder journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Cloud Application Builder product delivery journey": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The team compares runtime, database, API, messaging, and AI service paths against delivery speed and operational burden.

media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Cloud Application Builder product delivery journey": Evaluate.

stages.narrative: Developers compose managed services, configure secure access, and ship through cloud-native deployment paths.

media.title: Cloud Application Builder journey stage 3: Build
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Cloud Application Builder product delivery journey": Build.

stages.narrative: The team uses observability, cost, support, and documentation signals to improve reliability and economics.

media.title: Cloud Application Builder journey stage 4: Operate
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Cloud Application Builder product delivery journey": Operate.

stages.narrative: Builders stay and expand when the platform introduces higher-level services, better SDKs, service credits, predictable usage discounts, and migration paths that reduce toil for the next application or AI feature.

top.name: Cloud application release throughput
top.description: Reach 42 % increase in production releases per application team per quarter.
top.unit: % increase in production releases per application team per quarter

children.name: Golden path adoption
children.description: Increase the share of new services launched through approved cloud application paths.

children.name: Time to first deploy
children.description: Reduce median time from repository creation to first cloud deployment.

children.name: Deployment rollback rate
children.description: Reduce production releases requiring rollback or emergency fix-forward.

children.name: Observable service coverage
children.description: Increase services with logs, metrics, traces, and owner dashboards configured.

top.name: Managed application services attach
top.description: Reach 57 % of active application accounts using three or more managed platform services.
top.unit: % of active application accounts using three or more managed platform services

children.name: Runtime attach depth
children.description: Increase accounts using app runtime plus database plus integration services.

children.name: AI service attach
children.description: Increase application accounts using managed AI or analytics services.

children.name: Application workload retention
children.description: Increase retained usage among application-builder cohorts.

children.name: Support-assisted activation rate
children.description: Increase high-potential accounts completing production activation after support or solution guidance.

productStrategy.vision: Make public cloud the trusted execution environment for modern application, data, AI, and platform operations work.

1_year.focus: Make first production adoption easier with guided cloud paths, secure defaults, supportable operations, and measurable cost visibility.
strategicMilestones: Guided workload paths cover the highest-volume customer jobs
Secure landing-zone and cost controls become default setup steps
Customer support and docs reduce first-production abandonment

customerKPI.northStar: More teams launch governed workloads without bespoke platform work

businessKPI.northStar: Higher retained consumption through managed services, data, AI, and operations attach

3_year.focus: Expand from first workloads into managed platform consolidation across applications, data, AI, governance, and marketplace workflows.
strategicMilestones: Managed application, data, and AI services attach to most active accounts
Migration and marketplace workflows become repeatable enterprise expansion motions
FinOps, observability, and governance operate from shared telemetry

5_year.theme: Default operating model for digital workloads

customerKPI.northStar: Cloud becomes the normal way teams build, operate, secure, and optimize software
supporting: Cloud becomes default for new workloads

businessKPI.northStar: Durable cloud ecosystem growth across services, commitments, support, and marketplace
supporting: Higher ecosystem share in strategic accounts

5_year.productTheme: Default operating model for digital workloads
5_year.focus: Make public cloud the default operating environment for digital workloads, regulated adoption, AI services, and ecosystem-led expansion.
strategicMilestones: Customers treat cloud as the normal control plane for new and modernized workloads
Autonomous operations and policy-driven optimization reduce toil and cost leakage
Partner and sovereign ecosystems extend the platform into more regulated and regional markets

customers.name: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator
customers.description: A platform, SRE, security, or FinOps leader accountable for reliable, secure, and economically efficient cloud data and AI operations.
careAbout: Reliable landing zones, network patterns, identity, and policy controls for many teams
Fast detection of incidents, security drift, quota pressure, and cost anomalies
Cost allocation, commitment planning, and optimization across compute, storage, data, and AI usage
Clear evidence for architecture, risk, finance, and executive operating reviews
winsThem: Integrated observability, governance, security posture, and FinOps workflows
Automated policy guardrails that reduce manual review without blocking delivery
Reliable capacity, backup, recovery, and support paths for production workloads
Cost and usage telemetry that maps spend to owners, products, environments, and workloads
theirFear: Cloud usage expands faster than operating controls and finance accountability
A preventable outage, misconfiguration, or exposed credential damages trust
AI and accelerator demand creates capacity scarcity and unplanned cost spikes

jobsToBeDone.name: Operate cloud workloads with reliability and cost accountability
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Provision reliable environments, monitor workload health, allocate cost, detect anomalies, and guide teams toward efficient cloud usage.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: The organization can scale cloud adoption while keeping reliability, unit economics, and ownership visible.

steps.description: Create account, network, observability, and tagging patterns that workloads can inherit by default.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Virtual Networking
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides private networking, segmentation, DNS, and connectivity patterns.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Cloud Governance and Policy
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Applies policy, organization hierarchy, tagging, and landing-zone controls.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Identity and Access Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Ensures access follows federated identity and least-privilege operating models.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator JTBD step 1: Standardize operating foundations
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Operate cloud workloads with reliability and cost accountability": Standardize operating foundations.

steps.description: Track service health, usage, quotas, commitments, and anomalies across compute, storage, data, and AI workloads.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Monitoring and Observability
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides metrics, logs, traces, SLOs, dashboards, alerts, and incident workflows.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: FinOps and Commercial Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides budgets, forecasting, chargeback, commitments, and optimization recommendations.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Accelerated Compute
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Highlights accelerator capacity and high-cost AI workload behavior.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator JTBD step 2: Monitor health and cost signals
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Operate cloud workloads with reliability and cost accountability": Monitor health and cost signals.

steps.description: Use recovery, support, and optimization loops to reduce incidents and cost leakage over time.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Backup and Disaster Recovery
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides backup, replication, recovery orchestration, and continuity testing.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Enterprise Support and Advisory
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Supports escalations, architecture reviews, advisory, and operational guidance.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Migration and Modernization Factory
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Helps modernize inefficient or risky workloads into better cloud patterns.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator JTBD step 3: Recover and optimize continuously
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Operate cloud workloads with reliability and cost accountability": Recover and optimize continuously.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator JTBD infographic: Operate cloud workloads with reliability and cost accountability
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for the job "Operate cloud workloads with reliability and cost accountability" for customer "Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator".

jobsToBeDone.name: Keep cloud data and AI operations secure and audit-ready
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Control identity, keys, secrets, posture, data access, model-related infrastructure, and audit evidence across fast-growing cloud data and AI workloads.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Security, risk, and finance teams can approve cloud data and AI adoption without losing control of evidence, access, or economics.

steps.description: Set the identity, encryption, secrets, network, and policy baseline for regulated and high-risk workloads.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Identity and Access Management
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Controls user, role, service identity, and federation access.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Key Management and Encryption
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Manages encryption keys, HSM, rotation, and customer-managed key controls.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Secrets and Configuration
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Manages secrets and runtime configuration outside application code.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator JTBD step 1: Define security and compliance guardrails
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 1 of the job "Keep cloud data and AI operations secure and audit-ready": Define security and compliance guardrails.

steps.description: Continuously detect posture gaps, suspicious behavior, policy violations, and audit evidence gaps.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Threat Detection and Posture
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Detects threats, misconfiguration, vulnerability signals, and risky posture.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Monitoring and Observability
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Captures operational telemetry needed for incident response and audit reconstruction.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Cloud Governance and Policy
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Enforces policy, audit, compliance, and organization-level governance.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator JTBD step 2: Detect drift and risky behavior
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 2 of the job "Keep cloud data and AI operations secure and audit-ready": Detect drift and risky behavior.

steps.step: Operate controlled AI and data capacity
steps.description: Apply cost, capacity, and support controls to analytics, AI, and accelerator workloads that can grow quickly.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: Analytics and Data Platform
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Shows data platform usage and operational dependencies.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: AI and Machine Learning Platform
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Provides managed AI platform controls for model workloads.

capabilitiesNeeded.name: FinOps and Commercial Controls
capabilitiesNeeded.how_it_supports: Attributes spend and optimization opportunities across AI, analytics, and platform owners.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator JTBD step 3: Operate controlled AI and data capacity
media.alt: Enterprise infographic for step 3 of the job "Keep cloud data and AI operations secure and audit-ready": Operate controlled AI and data capacity.

customerJourneyStories.name: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator operating journey
customerJourneyStories.summary: A platform and FinOps operator standardizes cloud foundations, then runs reliability, security, and cost loops as usage expands across data and AI workloads.

stages.narrative: Cloud adoption, AI workloads, or accelerator spend grows faster than current operating controls can handle.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator journey stage 1: Trigger
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 1 of "Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator operating journey": Trigger.

stages.narrative: The operator defines landing zones, identity, network, logging, policy, and tagging standards.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator journey stage 2: Standardize
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 2 of "Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator operating journey": Standardize.

stages.narrative: Teams monitor SLOs, incidents, security findings, capacity, commitments, and cost anomalies through shared dashboards.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator journey stage 3: Operate
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 3 of "Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator operating journey": Operate.

stages.narrative: The organization improves workload patterns, commitment coverage, recovery posture, and policy automation.

media.title: Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator journey stage 4: Optimize
media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 4 of "Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator operating journey": Optimize.

stages.narrative: Operators renew and expand through reservation and savings-plan coverage, commitment true-ups, FinOps reviews, support plan tuning, security posture evidence, and new services that improve reliability per dollar.

media.alt: Enterprise journey panel for stage 5 of "Cloud Platform and FinOps Operator operating journey": Retain.

children.description: Increase resources passing automated policy checks.

children.name: Manual control evidence effort
children.description: Reduce monthly analyst hours spent assembling audit evidence.

customers.name: Startup CTO
customers.description: A technical founder or CTO using public cloud to launch quickly, experiment cheaply, and scale without upfront infrastructure investment.
careAbout: Fast setup, good docs, and low operational overhead
Elasticity from prototype to product-market fit
Access to managed data and AI services without hiring large platform teams
winsThem: Generous self-service paths and predictable early-stage costs
Production-ready primitives that do not require a long procurement cycle
Simple upgrade path from startup credits to committed usage
theirFear: Cloud bills outpace revenue before architecture stabilizes
Managed services become hard to unwind later
Small teams are overwhelmed by service complexity

productStrategy.vision: Make public cloud the fastest trusted path for customers to build, run, secure, and scale digital workloads.

1_year.focus: Make first production adoption easier with guided cloud paths, secure defaults, supportable operations, and measurable cost visibility.
strategicMilestones: Guided workload paths cover the highest-volume customer jobs
Secure landing-zone and cost controls become default setup steps
Customer support and docs reduce first-production abandonment

customerKPI.northStar: More workloads run on managed services with less operational toil

businessKPI.northStar: Higher retained consumption through platform, data, AI, and marketplace attach

3_year.focus: Expand from first workloads into managed platform consolidation across applications, data, AI, governance, and marketplace workflows.
strategicMilestones: Managed application, data, and AI services attach to most active accounts
Migration and marketplace workflows become repeatable enterprise expansion motions
FinOps, observability, and governance operate from shared telemetry

customerKPI.northStar: Cloud becomes the standard operating model for new and modernized workloads
supporting: Cloud becomes default for new workloads

businessKPI.northStar: Durable cloud ecosystem growth across services, commitments, support, and marketplace
supporting: Higher ecosystem share in strategic accounts

5_year.focus: Make public cloud the default operating environment for digital workloads, regulated adoption, AI services, and ecosystem-led expansion.
strategicMilestones: Customers treat cloud as the normal control plane for new and modernized workloads
Autonomous operations and policy-driven optimization reduce toil and cost leakage
Partner and sovereign ecosystems extend the platform into more regulated and regional markets

customers.name: Enterprise Cloud Transformation Leader
customers.description: A CIO, CTO, or cloud transformation leader accountable for portfolio modernization, cloud operating model, security, governance, and business value.
careAbout: Modernizing legacy systems without disrupting critical operations
Standardizing cloud landing zones, governance, and procurement
Showing measurable migration, resilience, and innovation outcomes
winsThem: Enterprise-grade migration support and architecture guidance
Commercial controls for commitments, chargeback, and marketplace use
theirFear: Security misconfiguration or outage creates board-level risk
Cloud spend grows faster than measurable business outcomes

jobsToBeDone.name: Modernize enterprise workloads with governance
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Move application portfolios into cloud foundations with landing zones, resilience, and policy controls.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Enterprise teams increase modernization throughput while reducing unmanaged cloud risk.

customers.name: Regulated Sector Platform Owner
customers.description: A platform or security leader in government, financial services, healthcare, telecom, or other regulated sectors adopting public cloud under strict control requirements.
careAbout: Data residency, encryption, identity, audit, and recovery controls
High availability for citizen, patient, financial, or critical business services
Clear evidence for regulators, auditors, and internal risk committees
winsThem: Strong identity, key, logging, and posture management
Support for hybrid, private connectivity, and staged modernization
theirFear: A control gap blocks production approval
Cloud concentration or outage risk is not acceptable
Auditors cannot trace who accessed what and why

jobsToBeDone.name: Adopt public cloud with audit-ready controls
jobsToBeDone.what_it_is: Provision secure, policy-compliant landing zones with identity, encryption, logging, and evidence from day one.
jobsToBeDone.outcome: Regulated workloads can be approved without bespoke control reconstruction.
